title: Lightly Spiced Moroccan Olive Stir-fry
description: This lazy-sunday dish features orange blossom water and tandoor spices to create a light and umami-rich olive stir-fry that's egg-free-friendly, perfect for incorporating into any meal plan.

ingredients.name: Olive Oranges (freshly squeezed juice of 3 oranges, plus rinds and peels)
Freshly Grated Parmesan Cheese (optional, for garnish)

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in turmeric powder and cumin seeds. Cook for another minute.
Toss in coriander seeds and continue to cook for about 30 seconds.
Pour in orange blossom water and let the mixture come to a gentle simmer.
Add freshly squeezed orange juice, rinds, and peels. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Reduce heat to low and gently fold in olive oil. Cook until mixture thickens slightly and flavors meld together, about 5 minutes more.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Sprinkle with fresh Parmesan cheese if desired.
Serve hot as a side dish or over rice.
